quiz.title: Unit 3 Quiz 3, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Metals, Nonmetals, and Metalloids
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Classification of Elements, Physical Properties of Metals and Nonmetals, Metalloids, Location on Periodic Table, Semiconductors, Reactivity Patterns

questions.question: Which of the following is a metal?
questions.explanation: Sodium (Na, Z=11) is an alkali metal in Group 1. Oxygen, sulfur, and bromine are nonmetals.

questions.question: Which element is a nonmetal?
questions.explanation: Chlorine (Cl, Z=17) is a halogen in Group 17 and a nonmetal. Iron, calcium, and nickel are metals.

questions.question: Which of the following is a metalloid?
questions.explanation: Boron (B, Z=5) is classified as a metalloid. It has properties intermediate between metals and nonmetals. Aluminum is a metal, lead is a metal, oxygen is a nonmetal.

questions.question: Nonmetals are usually:
questions.explanation: Nonmetals are typically brittle in solid form (they shatter when struck) or exist as gases at room temperature. They lack the malleability, luster, and conductivity of metals.

questions.question: Which metal is liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury (Hg) is the only metal that is liquid at standard room temperature (about 25°C). All other metals listed are solid at room temperature.

questions.question: Which nonmetal is liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Bromine (Br) is the only nonmetal that is liquid at room temperature. It is a reddish-brown volatile liquid.

questions.question: Which property is common in metals?
questions.explanation: Malleability (ability to be hammered into thin sheets) is a characteristic property of metals. They are also typically good conductors, have high melting points, and low electronegativity.

questions.question: Which property is common in nonmetals?
questions.explanation: Nonmetals tend to have high electronegativity (ability to attract electrons). They gain or share electrons in chemical reactions. They lack metallic luster, malleability, and ductility.

questions.question: Which of these is a transition metal?
questions.explanation: Iron (Fe, Z=26) is a transition metal located in Group 8, Period 4. Transition metals are characterized by the filling of d subshells. Sodium is an alkali metal (Group 1), oxygen and chlorine are nonmetals.

questions.question: Which element is sometimes called a semimetal?
questions.explanation: Silicon (Si) is a metalloid, also called a semimetal. It has properties intermediate between metals and nonmetals and is a key semiconductor material.

questions.question: Most metals are located:
questions.explanation: Most metals are found on the left side (Groups 1 and 2) and center (Groups 3–12) of the periodic table. Nonmetals are on the far right, and metalloids lie along the zigzag line.

questions.question: Which metalloid is used in semiconductors?
questions.explanation: Silicon (Si) is the most widely used metalloid in semiconductor devices. Its semiconducting properties make it essential for computer chips and electronics.

questions.question: Which of these is a poor conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Sulfur (S) is a nonmetal and a poor conductor of electricity. Copper, iron, and aluminum are metals and good conductors.

questions.question: Which of these elements is highly reactive with water?
questions.explanation: Potassium (K, Group 1) is highly reactive with water, producing potassium hydroxide and hydrogen gas with enough heat to ignite the hydrogen. Magnesium reacts slowly with hot water, aluminum forms a protective oxide, and copper does not react with water.

questions.question: Which property distinguishes metalloids from metals and nonmetals?
questions.explanation: Semi-conductivity (intermediate electrical conductivity) is a defining property of metalloids. They can conduct electricity better than nonmetals but not as well as metals, making them valuable in electronics.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the classification of metals, nonmetals, and metalloids! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the physical properties that distinguish metals from nonmetals.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the location of different element types on the periodic table.
scoreMessages.0: Please review metal, nonmetal, and metalloid properties and their positions on the periodic table.
